commit.message:
Fixes #4866. Add SetDefaultKeyBinding/RemoveDefaultKeyBinding methods (#4913)

* Initial plan

* Add SetDefaultKeyBinding/RemoveDefaultKeyBinding methods to Application

Fixes dictionary mutation not triggering ApplicationKeyboard bindings update.
Direct mutation of Application.DefaultKeyBindings dictionary bypasses the
property setter and never fires DefaultKeyBindingsChanged. The new methods
mutate the dictionary and raise the event in one atomic operation.

Updates existing KeyboardSetterTests to use SetDefaultKeyBinding instead of
calling internal keyboard.AddKeyBindings() workaround. Adds tests for event
firing, removal, and null dictionary edge case.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/6c7d5075-4105-474c-8c04-e5234d231816

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard DefaultKeyBindings assignment and null usage

Refactor Application.DefaultKeyBindings setter to avoid redundant updates by checking reference equality. Change SetDefaultKeyBinding to throw InvalidOperationException if DefaultKeyBindings is null, rather than initializing it. Update related tests to reflect and verify the new exception behavior and remove unused ApplicationKeyboard instance.

* Refactor KeyBindings layout and code style

Refactored ListView layout for clarity and alignment. Replaced target-typed new expressions with explicit types for consistency. Removed unnecessary ViewportSettingsFlags.HasVerticalScrollBar. Improved selection logic to prevent null reference issues. Cleaned up helper methods and variable initialization to match code style conventions.

FIFO sends raw bytes to the PTY which bypasses zellij's input routing.
TUI apps like copilot need zellij action write-chars + write 13 to
properly submit input. This is the same path the dashboard WebSocket
uses (which works). FIFO still used for shell sessions.

When the player reaches level 5 (full knight armor), a subtle ambient
particle effect activates — small glowing sparks drift off the character.

* fix: replace range-keyed pnpm overrides with plain keys; add .trivyignore for residual false positives

Trivy v0.69.3 misreads range-based pnpm override *keys* (e.g. `hono@<4.12.12`)
as installed package versions, creating new false-positive Code Scanning alerts
that differ from the base-branch fingerprints and cause the Trivy PR check to fail.

Fix:
- Convert five safe overrides to plain keys so Trivy reads the *patched* version
  directly: vite→7.3.2, hono→4.12.12, @hono/node-server→1.19.13, lodash→4.18.1,
  serialize-javascript→7.0.5
- Keep range-scoped keys for undici (avoids overriding @sentry/cli's undici@6.x),
  picomatch (2.x and 4.x sibling installs) and path-to-regexp (0.x/6.x/8.x)
- Add .trivyignore to suppress the residual false-positive CVE IDs that Trivy
  reports from those three necessary range keys
- Bump direct hono dep to ^4.12.12 in root and frontend package.json
- Regenerate pnpm-lock.yaml

commit.message:
fix: clear stale prefetched_list and validate index bounds on advance

Root cause: _prefetched_list was set by an early prefetch (e.g. queue
2→7 items) and never cleared when subsequent tracks advanced via branch
2a (direct advance). This blocked future prefetches and left a stale
list. When the last track ended (index 6/7), _signal_track_completion
used the stale 7-item list with next_index=7 → Ynison error 400030001.

Fixes:
- Clear _prefetched_list on direct advance (branch 2a) so _maybe_prefetch
  can re-trigger for the new queue tail
- Validate next_index < len(expanded) before sending to Ynison
- If expanded list is too short, re-fetch before giving up

commit.message:
feat(project-switch): add ProjectContextRebinder with rollback semantics (#135)

* feat(project-switch): add ProjectContextRebinder with rollback semantics

Implements P3-06 (Issue #134): Project Context Rebinding on Switch.

- Port interfaces: KgTrieCachePort, EpisodicMemoryCachePort, RebindableService
- Single composite lifecycle participant ('context-rebinder') registered with
  ProjectLifecycle, coordinating all context subsystem rebindings
- Rollback-on-bind-failure: on partial bind, unbind successfully-bound services
  from failed project (reverse order) then re-bind ALL to previous project
- 25 tests covering registration, unbind/bind ordering, rollback, optional
  services, async, tracking, logging, and edge cases
- LoggerPort exported for type-safe test mocking

* fix: guard lastBoundProjectId update against mid-loop signal abort

  to prevent partial bind from corrupting rollback target (INV-10)
- Add mid-loop abort test using getter-backed signal to cover the code path
  that was previously untested (pre-aborted signal only hit early-return)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: cleanup partially-bound services on initial bind failure

When bind fails and there is no previous project (initial bind), the
successfully-bound services were left dangling. Now cleanupPartialBind()
unbinds them in reverse order, preventing inconsistent partial state.

Addresses Duck R2 finding #2.

Duck R2 findings #1 (multi-scope rollback) and #3 (integration test with
real lifecycle timeout) are dismissed as out-of-scope for P3-06:
- #1: ProjectLifecycle multi-scope is infrastructure-level; no current code
  exercises concurrent multi-scope switching
- #3: Unit tests cover abort logic; integration tests are beyond this PR

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: abort path cleanup + orphaned JSDoc + scope constraint doc

R3 audit findings (Duck + Sonnet):

1. BLOCKING (Duck): When signal.aborted fires mid-loop during bind,
   the break exits without cleaning up partially-bound services. Now
   triggers rollback (if previous project) or cleanupPartialBind (if
   initial bind) — same recovery as the catch path.

2. NIT (Sonnet): Orphaned JSDoc block for rollback() was separated
   from its target function by the cleanupPartialBind insertion.
   Moved JSDoc to directly precede rollback().

3. NON-BLOCKING (Duck): Added scope constraint documentation in
   module header explaining single-scope assumption and future
   extension path for multi-scope.

Tests: 28 (was 26) — added abort-with-rollback and abort-with-cleanup
scenarios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
